name: The Citizens State Bank At Mohall
description_short: Community bank with three North Dakota locations offering traditional banking services, online/mobile access, and a surcharge-free ATM network.
description_long:
Citizens State Bank at Mohall is an independent community bank operating in rural North Dakota with a presence in Mohall, Sherwood, and Lansford. The bank was acquired by Jorgenson Holding Company in 1989 and is a member of the Independent Community Banks of North Dakota organization. As part of a larger holding company that also owns State Bank & Trust of Kenmare, the institution operates with institutional backing while maintaining local decision-making. The bank offers comprehensive retail banking services including checking and savings accounts, credit cards, and various transaction services designed for consumers and small businesses in its service areas.

The bank's service offerings include standard deposit products, Visa debit and ATM cards, online and mobile banking platforms, wire transfers, ACH services, direct deposit, safe deposit boxes, and a 24-hour night depository. Customers can access accounts around the clock through the mobile app and online banking portal. The bank participates in the MoneyPass nationwide surcharge-free ATM network, providing access beyond its three physical locations. Additional services include notary services, cashier's checks, bank-by-mail options, and automatic transfer capabilities. The institution also offers credit cards and insurance products, though specific details about these offerings are limited on the website.

What distinguishes Citizens State Bank at Mohall is its deep community integration in three small North Dakota towns and its participation in the surcharge-free MoneyPass ATM network, which extends customer convenience beyond rural North Dakota. The bank emphasizes modern digital banking access with mobile and online platforms, positioning itself as accessible 24/7. Its status as an independent community bank member suggests a commitment to local lending and relationship banking rather than national corporate operations. The institution's slogan "We Have a Banker For You" suggests personalized service orientation.

For consumers, the main limitation is geographic reach—the bank operates only in three North Dakota locations, making it exclusively relevant to residents in Mohall, Sherwood, Lansford, and nearby areas. The website provides minimal detail about specific account features, fee structures, interest rates, or loan products, requiring direct contact for substantive product information. As a small community bank, it may have more limited product sophistication compared to regional or national banks, though this can be offset by personalized local service and decision-making.
pros: Nationwide surcharge-free ATM access through MoneyPass network despite three-location footprint
24/7 mobile and online banking access for remote account management
24-hour night depository for after-hours deposits at main Mohall location
Electronic statements (eStatements) available for paperless banking and convenience
Member of Independent Community Banks of North Dakota organization
Multiple convenient services including wire transfers, ACH, direct deposit, and safe deposit boxes
Notary services available in-branch for customer convenience
cons: Limited to three small North Dakota towns (Mohall, Sherwood, Lansford) with no broader geographic presence
Website provides minimal detail on account types, fees, interest rates, and loan products
No information about business lending, mortgage services, or specialized products available online
Small institution may have limited product offerings compared to regional or national competitors
Requires phone contact or in-person visit to obtain detailed pricing and product information
best_for: Residents of Mohall, Sherwood, and Lansford, North Dakota seeking local community banking
Small business owners in rural North Dakota needing relationship-based banking services
Consumers prioritizing personalized local service over national bank convenience
Rural North Dakotans wanting 24/7 digital banking access with local branch relationships
diagnosis: Citizens State Bank at Mohall is best for residents of three small North Dakota communities (Mohall, Sherwood, Lansford) seeking traditional banking services with local decision-making and 24/7 digital access. The main caveat is severe geographic limitation—the bank serves only a rural three-town area, making it irrelevant for anyone outside this region, and the website lacks transparent pricing and product details, requiring direct contact for substantive information.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
